term_inventory_greek.meaning: gentleness, meekness, humility; also spelled praotēs: meekness, mildness, forbearance, gentleness (1Pet. 3:15); gentleness, kindness, humility (Jas. 1:21; 3:13; Gal. 5:23)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: praotēs, ētos, hē — mildness, gentleness; opposed to agriotēs; opposed to orgilotēs; opposed to orgē: later prautēs [LXX+NT]

term_inventory_greek.meaning: humility, humbleness, modesty; lowliness, or humility of mind, modesty (Acts 20:19; Eph. 4:2; Phil. 2:3; Col. 2:18, 23; 3:12; 1Pet. 5:5)

gentle, meek — the positive moral quality of dealing with people in a kind manner, with humility and consideration; also spelled praos: meek, gentle, kind, forgiving (Mt. 5:5); mild, benevolent, humane, humble, considerate (Mt. 11:29; 21:5; 1Pet. 3:4)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: praos, on (also praus, Ionic prēus, eia, u): mild, soft, gentle. (1) Of things: mild, soft, gentle — of sound, soft, gentle; of climate, mild. (2) Of persons: mild, gentle, meek; especially after having been angry; of a horse, gentle; of other animals, tame. (3) Of actions, feelings: mild, gentle. Adverb praotos — mildly, gently. Cognate with Sanskrit prinatī (love, give pleasure to), Gothic frijon (love), frijonds (friend).

term_inventory_greek.meaning: humble, lowly, downcast, timid; low in situation; of condition: humble, poor, mean, depressed, downcast (Lk. 1:52; 2Cor. 7:6; Jas. 1:9); metaphorically of the mind: humble, lowly, timid, modest (Mt. 11:29; Rom. 12:16; 2Cor. 10:1; Jas. 4:6; 1Pet. 5:5)
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: tapeinos, ē, on — low. (1) Of place: low-lying. (2) Of persons: humbled, abased in power, pride; submissive; small, poor, weak (of cities and states). (3) Of the spirits: downcast, dejected. (4) In moral sense: either bad — mean, base, abject; or good — lowly, humble (frequently in NT).

verse_records.verse_text: to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show perfect courtesy toward all people.
Therefore put away all filthiness and rampant wickedness and receive with meekness the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom.
but in your hearts honor Christ the Lord as holy, always being prepared to make a defense to anyone who asks you for a reason for the hope that is in you; yet do it with gentleness and respect,
You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your gentleness made me great.
The fear of the Lord is instruction in wisdom, and humility comes before honor.
Before destruction a man's heart is haughty, but humility comes before honor.
The reward for humility and fear of the Lord is riches and honor and life.
Seek the Lord, all you humble of the land, who do his just commands; seek righteousness; seek humility; perhaps you may be hidden on the day of the anger of the Lord.
You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your right hand supported me, and your gentleness made me great.
In your majesty ride out victoriously for the cause of truth and meekness and righteousness; let your right hand teach you awesome deeds!
When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom.
serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials that happened to me through the plots of the Jews;
with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
Let no one disqualify you, insisting on asceticism and worship of angels, going on in detail about visions, puffed up without reason by his sensuous mind,
These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body, but they are of no value in stopping the indulgence of the flesh.
Put on then, as God's chosen ones, holy and beloved, compassionate hearts, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience,
Likewise, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. Clothe yourselves, all of you, with humility toward one another, for 'God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.'
The Lord makes poor and makes rich; he brings low and he exalts.
You save a humble people, but your eyes are on the haughty to bring them down.
For when they are humbled you say, 'It is because of pride'; but he saves the lowly.
Pour out the overflowings of your anger, and look on everyone who is proud and abase him.
For you save a humble people, but the haughty eyes you bring down.
but it is God who executes judgment, putting down one and lifting up another.
who looks far down on the heavens and the earth?
The Lord lifts up the humble; he casts the wicked to the ground.
It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor than to divide the spoil with the proud.
for it is better to be told, 'Come up here,' than to be put lower in the presence of a noble.
One's pride will bring him low, but he who is lowly in spirit will obtain honor.
and the doors on the street are shut — when the sound of the grinding is low, and one rises up at the sound of a bird, and all the daughters of song are brought low —
So man is humbled, and each one is brought low — do not forgive them!
The haughty looks of man shall be brought low, and the lofty pride of men shall be humbled, and the Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
For the Lord of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty, against all that is lifted up — and it shall be brought low;
And the haughtiness of man shall be humbled, and the lofty pride of men shall be brought low, and the Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
Man is humbled, and each one is brought low, and the eyes of the haughty are brought low.
Behold, the Lord God of hosts will lop the boughs with terrifying power; the great in height will be hewn down, and the lofty will be brought low.
I will punish the world for its evil, and the wicked for their iniquity; I will put an end to the pomp of the arrogant, and lay low the pompous pride of the ruthless.
And he will spread out his hands in the midst of it as a swimmer spreads his hands out to swim, but the Lord will lay low his pompous pride together with the skill of his hands.
And the high fortifications of his walls he will bring down, lay low, and cast to the ground, to the dust.
For he has humbled the inhabitants of the height, the lofty city. He lays it low, lays it low to the ground, casts it to the dust.

And you will be brought low; from the earth you shall speak, and from the dust your speech will be bowed down; your voice shall come from the ground like the voice of a ghost, and from the dust your speech shall whisper.
And it will hail when the forest falls down, and the city will be utterly laid low.
Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
You journeyed to the king with oil and multiplied your perfumes; you sent your envoys far off, and sent down even to Sheol.
Say to the king and the queen mother: 'Take a lowly seat, for your beautiful crown has come down from your head.'

And all the trees of the field shall know that I am the Lord; I bring low the high tree, and make high the low tree, dry up the green tree, and make the dry tree flourish. I am the Lord; I have spoken, and I will do it.
thus says the Lord God: Remove the turban and take off the crown. Things shall not remain as they are. Exalt that which is low, and bring low that which is exalted.
Now the man Moses was very meek, more than all people who were on the face of the earth.
They thrust the poor off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
For he who avenges blood is mindful of them; he does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
Arise, O Lord; O God, lift up your hand; forget not the afflicted.
O Lord, you hear the desire of the afflicted; you will strengthen their heart; you will incline your ear
The afflicted shall eat and be satisfied; those who seek him shall praise the Lord! May your hearts live forever!
He leads the humble in what is right, and teaches the humble his way.
My soul makes its boast in the Lord; let the humble hear and be glad.
But the meek shall inherit the land and delight themselves in abundant peace.
When the humble see it they will be glad; you who seek God, let your hearts revive.
when God arose to establish judgment, to save all the humble of the earth. Selah
The Lord lifts up the humble; he casts the wicked to the ground.
For the Lord takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with salvation.
Toward the scorners he is scornful, but to the humble he gives favor.
Whoever despises his neighbor is a sinner, but blessed is he who is generous to the poor.
It is better to be of a lowly spirit with the poor than to divide the spoil with the proud.

but with righteousness he shall judge the poor, and decide with equity for the meek of the earth; and he shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall kill the wicked.
The meek shall obtain fresh joy in the Lord, and the poor among mankind shall exult in the Holy One of Israel.

The Spirit of the Lord God is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me to bring good news to the poor; he has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to those who are bound;
those who trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth and turn aside the way of the afflicted; a man and his father go in to the same girl, so that my holy name is profaned;
Seek the Lord, all you humble of the land, who do his just commands; seek righteousness; seek humility; perhaps you may be hidden on the day of the anger of the Lord.
Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
Say to the daughter of Zion, 'Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a beast of burden.'
But as for you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, steadfastness, gentleness.
but let your adorning be the hidden person of the heart with the imperishable beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which in God's sight is very precious.
Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
he has brought down the mighty from their thrones and exalted those of humble estate;
Live in harmony with one another. Do not be haughty, but associate with the lowly. Never be wise in your own sight.
But God, who comforts the downcast, comforted us by the coming of Titus,
I, Paul, myself entreat you, by the meekness and gentleness of Christ — I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away! —
Let the lowly brother boast in his exaltation,
But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, 'God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.'
Likewise, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. Clothe yourselves, all of you, with humility toward one another, for 'God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.'

cross_registry_links.note: sha.phel appears in explicit antithesis to pride in multiple Isaiah passages (Isa 2:11, 2:17, 5:15, 13:11) and in Proverbs (Pro 16:19, 29:23). The state sha.phel describes is frequently the result of pride being broken.
a.na.vah appears as the explicit antithesis of pride in Pro 15:33 and Pro 18:12: 'humility comes before honor' set against the proud heart.
praus is used in Mat 5:5 (beatitude of the meek) — the same verse cited in the STEP Bible suggestion for a.nav (Ps 37:11 OT antecedent). A cross-registry link exists through the beatitude tradition.

The ANAV root family shares the root a.nah (to afflict) with the grief/affliction word cluster. a.nav's range includes 'afflicted' (sub-definition 1c) and the related words list includes a.ni (afflicted) and o.ni (affliction), which may appear in the grief registry.

data_quality_flags.description: sha.phel has an occurrence count of approximately 36. The source file records 22 verses. 22 of 36 = 61% — this exceeds the 20% threshold, so SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE does not formally apply. However, the verse set is dominated by prophetic judgement contexts (particularly Isaiah). Other registers may be under-represented. Noted for Phase 2 review.
a.nav has an occurrence count of approximately 22. The source file records 20 verses. 20 of 22 = 91% — SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE does not apply. Coverage is near-complete by count. Noted for completeness.

tsa.nu.a (H6800B) has an occurrence count of approximately 1. Only one verse is recorded (Pro 11:2). The related word tsa.na is listed but has no separate word analysis block in the source file. Meaning limited to 'adj — modest'. Register assessment is necessarily very limited.

The file header lists tapeinofrosunē under the primary term heading 'humility (tapeinofrosunē - tapeinophrosune)' with slightly different transliteration spelling (tapeinofrosunē vs tapeinophrosunē). Both refer to G5012. This is a transliteration variant, not a missing term. Resolved: treated as the same term throughout.

a.na.vah (H6038) and an.vah (H6037) are listed as alternate spellings of each other (each entry names the other as 'also spelled'). They share identical related-word clusters, identical occurrence count (both 'about 7'), and nearly identical meaning blocks. The verse sets are largely distinct (a.na.vah: 2Sa 22:36, Pro 15:33, 18:12, 22:4, Zep 2:3; an.vah: Psa 18:35, 45:4), suggesting they may be assigned to different occurrences of the same underlying word rather than being meaningfully distinct terms. Flagged for Phase 2 consolidation assessment.

Col 2:18 and Col 2:23 are listed as verse records for tapeinofrosunē in the source file, but the verse texts do not contain the word 'humility' or any obvious cognate. These may be included in STEP because tapeinofrosunē appears in the broader pericope (Col 2:18–23 context) or because they illustrate a negative/false form of humility. Recorded as supplied in the source. Phase 2 should verify the exact lexical occurrence.
